evals.prompt: Create a spec for a feature that lets users schedule recurring exports of their account data (daily/weekly/monthly) with email delivery.
evals.expected_output: SAGE dispatches SCOOP for research, captures intent in its own words, drafts a spec.md under artifacts/spec###-recurring-exports/ covering Overview, P1/P2 user scenarios with acceptance criteria, FR-### requirements table, SC-### success criteria, Edge Cases, and Open Questions. File is written via create_file. SAGE reports back with folder path, files written, 1-2 sentence summary, and Open Questions count. Does NOT begin a plan.
expectations: SCOOP is dispatched for technical research before the spec is drafted
Spec is written to disk via create_file at artifacts/spec###-<short-name>/spec.md, not returned as response text
Spec contains all required sections: Overview, User Scenarios (P1 + P2 minimum), Requirements (FR-### table), Success Criteria (SC-###), Edge Cases, Open Questions
Open Questions section is explicit (lists questions or says "None") — never silently omitted
Handoff report includes folder path, file(s) written, brief summary, and Open Questions count; SAGE does not begin a plan

evals.prompt: I want a way for my users to 'collaborate better'. Create a spec for it.
evals.expected_output: SAGE restates the request in its own words, identifies the ambiguity (what kind of collaboration? comments? shared editing? notifications?), and either asks the user for clarification before proceeding or drafts a spec with a prominent Open Questions section capturing the ambiguity. Does not fabricate a specific collaboration feature as if the user had specified it.
expectations: SAGE captures intent by restating the request before drafting any structure
Ambiguity is surfaced explicitly — either as a clarifying question to the user or in the spec's Open Questions section
SAGE does NOT invent specific scope (e.g., "real-time co-editing with presence indicators") without user confirmation
If a draft spec is produced, the Open Questions section is substantial and flagged at the Spec Checkpoint handoff
SAGE reports Open Questions count to ARTHUR so the human checkpoint can resolve them before planning

evals.prompt: Create a spec comparing Postgres vs MySQL for our workload.
evals.expected_output: SAGE recognizes this as a research request rather than a feature spec, declines to produce a spec.md, and recommends routing to SCOOP for a comparison. If a migration spec would be warranted after research, SAGE offers to produce one once the research returns.
expectations: SAGE does NOT write a spec.md for a research/comparison question
SAGE identifies the request as research-shaped and recommends SCOOP instead
No artifacts/spec###-*/ folder is created for this request
SAGE clearly explains the distinction: specs are for building something, research returns findings in-conversation
SAGE offers a clear path forward (e.g., "after SCOOP's comparison, I can spec a migration if you choose one")
